name: Weights & Biases API
what_it_does: Tracks ML experiments, manages model artifacts and registries, and provides monitoring for model training runs via REST API and Python SDK.
use_cases: Log training metrics and hyperparameters from automated ML pipelines without human intervention
Pull experiment results via REST API to compare runs and select the best-performing model checkpoint
Trigger downstream workflows via webhooks when a training run completes or a model artifact is published
Manage artifact versions in a model registry to promote models from staging to production programmatically
Run hyperparameter sweeps (W&B Sweeps) coordinated by an agent across distributed training jobs
not_for: Serving or hosting ML models for inference (use a dedicated inference platform)
General-purpose data storage or database workloads outside of ML artifacts
Real-time low-latency event streaming or observability for non-ML applications
best_when: Best when an AI agent needs to orchestrate and monitor long-running ML training pipelines and must programmatically compare runs, retrieve artifacts, or trigger downstream actions on completion.
avoid_when: Avoid when you only need simple file storage for model weights with no experiment metadata — S3 or GCS is cheaper and simpler.

auth.notes: Single API key per user; key is passed via WANDB_API_KEY environment variable or wandb.login(). Team/org workspaces are accessed via the same key scoped to the entity. No fine-grained scopes — the key has full account access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unlimited tracked experiments for personal use; 100 GB artifact storage free; limited team seats and private projects
pricing.paid_tiers: Team plan from ~$50/user/month; Enterprise pricing available with SSO, audit logs, and dedicated support
pricing.notes: Free tier is generous for individual and research use. Storage overages are billed. Artifact storage beyond free tier is ~$0.08/GB/month.

performance.rate_limits: REST API rate limits not publicly documented; SDK batches metric logs internally. GraphQL API subject to undocumented per-org limits. Sweeps agents poll on ~5s intervals.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK exceptions are descriptive but HTTP error bodies from the REST/GraphQL API are often minimal JSON with a generic message field. Agents need to handle HTTP 429 and 5xx with exponential backoff as the SDK does not always do this transparently.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Run creation is idempotent if you pass a fixed run ID (id= parameter). Artifact logging is append-only; re-logging the same artifact version creates a new version rather than overwriting. Metric logging is always append-only.
known_agent_gotchas: API key has full account scope — leaking it in logs or environment dumps exposes all projects and artifacts with no way to limit blast radius
wandb.finish() must be called explicitly at the end of a run; if an agent crashes without calling it, the run stays in 'running' state and must be manually killed via the UI or API
Artifact download URLs are pre-signed S3/GCS URLs with short expiry (~1 hour); agents caching these URLs will get 403 errors on subsequent requests
The public REST API is underdocumented — most programmatic access relies on the Python SDK internals or the GraphQL API, which can change between SDK releases without a formal versioning guarantee
Sweeps controller runs locally or in the cloud but requires a persistent process; if the sweep agent process dies, in-flight runs are orphaned and must be manually stopped

security_score_components.security_notes: API key provides full account access with no scoping — a significant risk for automated agents. No IP allowlisting on free/team tiers. Artifact access URLs are pre-signed and time-limited, which is good. SOC2 Type II certified.
